1.1: I, the elder, am writing to the chosen lady and her children. I love all of you genuinely—and so does everyone who knows the truth—
1.5: And now, dear lady, I want to remind you of something—this isn't a new command, just the one we have always had: that we love one another.
1.3: Grace, mercy, and peace will be ours, given by God the Father and Jesus Christ, the Father's Son, through truth and love.
1.6: And this is what love means: that we live according to his commands. And the command—as you've known from the beginning—is that you live in love.
1.13: The children of your sister—God's chosen one—send their love.
1.2: because of the truth that lives in us and will be with us forever.
1.4: I was overjoyed to find some of your children living in the truth, just as the Father has commanded us.
1.12: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to put it in writing. I hope to come and talk with you in person—that will make our joy complete.
1.8: Be careful that you don't throw away what we have worked for—you want to receive your full reward.
1.7: Watch out—many deceivers have gone out into the world. They deny that Jesus Christ came as a real human being. Anyone like this is a deceiver and an antichrist.
1.10: If someone comes to you who does not hold to this teaching, don't invite them in or welcome them.
1.11: Anyone who welcomes them becomes a partner in their evil work.
1.9: Anyone who goes beyond and does not remain in the teaching of Christ does not have God. But whoever remains in that teaching has both the Father and the Son.
